masked_text:
[ORG] [LOCATION]
    Dependents are defined as the employee’s spouse and/or unmarried children [DATE].
    [ORG] may cover the costs for dependents’ visa status or work authorization, but [NAME] a case-by-case basis.
    If [ORG] chooses not to cover dependent costs, the employee will be notified as soon as possible so they can prepare the necessary documents.
    Dependents can still use [ORG]’s immigration attorney [LOCATION] fee rate negotiated for the company, even if [ORG] does not cover their costs.
    For visa stamping at a [ORG], [ORG] will cover the costs for dependents only [LOCATION] (e.g., [ORG], E-3, [ORG], H-1B1), but not for travel expenses.
    If a visa stamp is needed after USCIS approval, [ORG] will not cover consulate visa costs for dependents, but the discounted attorney rate still [PERSON].
